Real estate, handled the disciplined way.

Born and raised in Colorado Springs. Nine years, 136 closings, $48.9M sold — and the same plan Ramsey teaches, run on my own money first.

Credentials

Ramsey does not hand this out. You have to keep earning it.

Endorsed Local Providers are vetted on service, held to Ramsey’s standards, and reviewed on what clients actually say afterward. That is the filter you just came through. Behind it is a Colorado Springs brokerage, a nine-year local record, and one person accountable for the result.

RamseyTrusted Certified ELP

Track record

Two journeys

Tell me which side of the table you're on.

Buying and selling are different problems. So the advice is different, and the plan is different. Pick yours.

Run it yourself

What can you actually afford —
and where does that reach?

Built on the rule I'd hold you to anyway: a payment at or under 25% of your monthly take-home, on a 15-year fixed. No email required to see the answer.

$ 6,200 / month

What actually lands in your account after taxes.

$ 40,000

Loan term

At 25% of take-home, you can buy up to

$0

$0 per month, all in — that's 25% of your take-home.

Principal & interest $0
Property taxes (est.) $0
Homeowners insurance (est.) $0
Total monthly payment $0

What this calculation assumes

Payment capped at 25% of monthly take-home pay, per the Ramsey rule.

Property taxes and homeowners insurance are estimated as a share of home value and scale with price. Colorado Springs insurance runs above the national average because of hail exposure.

Rates shown are assumptions, not quotes. Assumptions last reviewed: —.

No HOA dues, PMI, or mortgage insurance are included. Many Colorado Springs communities carry HOA dues, which reduce what you can afford.

Where that number reaches

Jake, is this the right number for me?

Send that exact question, with your numbers already attached. No form, no funnel.

Text me this question

These are estimates for planning, not a lending decision, a pre-approval, or a mortgage quote. Actual figures depend on your credit, the property, current rates, and your lender's underwriting.
